_comment: Canon self-adoption config (FT-23). Wave 0 self-adopts before Wave 1+ consumers pull, per CLAUDE.md. dry_run: true — this reports only; graduate to live after a run of zero-divergence reports, the same policy consumers follow.

changelog_stub._comment: Canon's CHANGELOG header is '## Unreleased' (NOT operations' '## [Unreleased]') — see CHANGELOG.md:6. Trigger paths are the surfaces whose changes are release-relevant and per this repo's own canon-source discipline must ship with a CHANGELOG entry: the reusables, the shipped templates, the canonical scripts, and the rulebook.

version_sync._comment: DISABLED deliberately, not by oversight. Canon already propagates VERSION with scripts/sync-version-refs.sh, which is wired as a pre-commit hook AND asserted by tests/test_version_sync.sh — a deterministic mechanism that runs before a commit exists, strictly stronger than a post-merge AI pass. Enabling this op would duplicate it and could contradict it. Revisit only if the canonical script is retired.

cross_ref_repair._comment: alpha.1 stub upstream — keep disabled until the implementation lands (same posture as the operations pilot).
